title: He’s running Boston to support people like him with Type 1 diabetes
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Collin Bennett, 29, from New Braintree, Mass., is running the Boston Marathon for Team Beth Israel Lahey Health.
Diagnosed with Type 1 Diabetes at 11, Bennett supports the Joslin Diabetes Center, which gave him hope and guidance.
Bennett runs to support diabetes research and for the 11-year-old boy who thought running 26.2 miles was impossible.

title: Springfield native Ruth E. Carter keeps making Oscars history
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Ruth E. Carter, from Springfield, is the most-nominated Black woman in Oscar history with her fifth nod for 'Sinners.'
Carter won Oscars for Best Costume Design in 2019 and 2023, becoming the first Black person to win in this category.
She designs her own dresses and sources costumes with a team, using places like Brimfield Marketplace in Massachusetts.

title: Brookline schools would see more than 200 layoffs if override fails, officials say
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Brookline schools face over 200 layoffs if a proposed property-tax override isn't approved, officials warn.
The district needs $18.5 million over three years to maintain current staffing and programs, report states.
Brookline voters to decide on the override at the May 5 annual town election, amid rising costs and limited revenue growth.

title: What to know about the PETS Act aimed at protecting dogs and cats in Mass.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: The PETS Act would ban pet shop sales of dogs and cats to curb puppy mill sourcing in Massachusetts.
The bill promotes partnerships between pet stores and shelters for adoptions, enhancing animal welfare.
It addresses public housing and insurance issues, allowing broader pet ownership access in Massachusetts.

title: As snow melts, drought still a big issue for Mass.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Massachusetts regions placed under Critical and Significant Drought levels despite heavy February snowfall.
Cold temperatures hinder snowmelt, resulting in insufficient groundwater replenishment across the state.
State officials urge water conservation and propose using WaterSense products and tools to manage usage.

title: Why some women are hiding their boyfriends online
author: Meredith Goldstein
bullets: Chanté Joseph discusses why some women are hiding their boyfriends online in the Love Letters podcast.
Joseph argues women are rethinking traditional heterosexual relationships to maintain independence and identity.
Her viral essay inspired debates and artistic interpretations, highlighting shifts in societal norms for relationships.

title: He’s running the Boston Marathon for a camp ‘where illness takes a back seat to fun’
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Griffin O'Neill, 27, is running the Boston Marathon to support The Hole in the Wall Gang Camp.
The camp offers children with serious medical conditions a chance to enjoy activities in a medically safe environment.
O'Neill's fiancée's sister, a former camper, is now a counselor there after overcoming bone cancer.

title: Eagles reportedly ‘will not trade A.J. Brown at this time’ amid Patriots’ continued interest
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Eagles are not trading A.J. Brown to the Patriots at this time, despite strong pursuit from New England.
Trade discussions for A.J. Brown may resume near June 1 when salary cap relief becomes available for the Eagles.
New England Patriots offered first- and third-round picks for A.J. Brown, but the Eagles reportedly rejected the proposal.

title: Jayson Tatum set to return vs. Wizards after injury management off-day
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jayson Tatum will return for the Celtics against the Wizards after managing his right Achilles injury.
Tatum has been on a minutes restriction since returning from a ruptured Achilles, playing 27 minutes each game.
Celtics' Baylor Scheierman and Derrick White are probable, while Nikola Vucevic is out with a finger fracture.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'War Machine' starring Alan Ritchson is now available for streaming on Netflix.
'Zootopia 2' with Jason Bateman and Ginnifer Goodwin is available on Disney+.
'Rooster', a comedy starring Steve Carell, is streaming on HBO Max.
